nodes.title: GPT-4 Technical Report
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the development of large multimodal models by presenting the capabilities and limitations of GPT-4
nodes.concepts: multimodal model, scaling laws, human-level performance

nodes.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
nodes.authors: Touvron, Hugo, Lavril, Thibaut, Izacard, Gautier
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the field by introducing publicly available, efficient, and open foundation language models that can be used by others.
nodes.concepts: foundation language models, large language models, democratization of access

nodes.title: Llama 2: Open Foundation and Fine-Tuned Chat Models
nodes.authors: Touvron, Hugo, Martin, Louis, Stone, Kevin
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the development and release of pretrained and fine-tuned large language models for improved dialogue use cases
nodes.concepts: large language models, fine-tuning, dialogue use cases

nodes.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
nodes.authors: Brown, Tom, Mann, Benjamin, Ryder, Nick
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the understanding of language models' capabilities in few-shot learning and challenges traditional approaches to NLP task-specific datasets
nodes.concepts: few-shot learning, in-context learning, language models

nodes.title: Mistral 7B
nodes.authors: Jiang, Albert Q., Sablayrolles, Alexandre, Mensch, Arthur
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes a new state-of-the-art language model, Mistral 7B, that outperforms existing models in terms of performance and efficiency
nodes.concepts: language model, grouped-query attention, sliding window attention

nodes.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
nodes.authors: Wei, Jason, Wang, Xuezhi, Schuurmans, Dale
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the understanding of how chain-of-thought prompting can improve the ability of large language models to perform complex reasoning tasks
nodes.concepts: chain-of-thought prompting, large language models, complex reasoning

nodes.title: Training language models to follow instructions with human feedback
nodes.authors: Ouyang, Long, Wu, Jeff, Jiang, Xu
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the development of more aligned and effective language models by demonstrating the potential of fine-tuning with human feedback
nodes.concepts: language models, reinforcement learning, human feedback

nodes.title: Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code
nodes.authors: Chen, Mark, Tworek, Jerry, Jun, Heewoo
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the development of code generation technologies by introducing Codex, a fine-tuned GPT language model that can solve a significant portion of HumanEval problems
nodes.concepts: Large Language Models, Code Generation, GPT

nodes.title: Augmented Language Models: a Survey
nodes.authors: Mialon, Grégoire, Dessì, Roberto, Lomeli, Maria
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes a survey of works on augmented language models, highlighting their potential to overcome fundamental limitations.

nodes.title: Are Emergent Abilities of Large Language Models a Mirage?
nodes.authors: Schaeffer, Rylan, Miranda, Brando, Koyejo, Sanmi
nodes.contribution: This paper contributes to the understanding of large language models by challenging the narrative of emergent abilities and phase transitions, and highlighting the importance of metric choice in evaluating model capabilities
nodes.concepts: emergent abilities, large language models, phase transitions
